summary: Re-tested full backend pipeline + frontend after switch to openai gpt-5-nano via emergentintegrations. ALL 5 backend pytest cases PASS including end-to-end multi-agent pipeline (~1m45s) - all 7 agent_* fields populated. Multi-tenant isolation verified (auth2 gets 404). Frontend AnalysisView renders all 8 data-testid cards with real agent data (no 'No data'); script tabs balanced/high_viral/risk_controlled all switch content correctly; dashboard nav works. Pipeline flow end-to-end is GREEN.

minor.issue: Backend becomes intermittently unresponsive (502 Bad Gateway / read timeouts) WHILE a pipeline is running concurrently. Tests pass with retry/longer timeouts but indicates litellm/emergentintegrations send_message is partially blocking the FastAPI event loop, or 6 parallel LLM calls saturate the single uvicorn worker. Once pipeline finishes, the service recovers. Not a correctness bug but a concurrency/UX concern (other users' requests will time out during a run).

issues: Recharts ResponsiveContainer console warning: 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0'. Chart still renders visibly but parent container reports negative dimensions on initial mount. Consider setting explicit minHeight on the chart wrapper.

action_items: (Optional/perf) Investigate why backend stalls on other API requests while pipeline runs - likely litellm sync internals or Anthropic-style rate burst. Consider running run_pipeline in a separate worker/process or asyncio.to_thread per agent call.
(Minor) Fix Recharts virality container dimension warning by giving wrapper a min-height.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py uses sync `requests.get` inside async exchange_session_id - blocks event loop during Google session exchange. Use httpx.AsyncClient.
agents.py runs 6 LLM calls via asyncio.gather - good - but if litellm has any sync path it will starve other requests; verified empirically that root/list endpoints 502 during pipeline runs.
server.py stores doc with isoformat(created_at) string while Analysis model defines datetime - inconsistent typing for downstream sort/filter.
EMERGENT_LLM_KEY captured at module load into a constant; agents.py reads via os.environ.get at call time (good); but server.py's STT path uses the constant - fine since same module reload.

success_rate.backend: 100% (5/5 pytest, full pipeline incl. all 7 agents)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 8 cards render real data, all 3 script tabs work, dashboard nav works)

test_credentials: Sessions seeded via mongosh into test_database.user_sessions per /app/auth_testing.md. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in /app/backend/.env confirmed working with openai gpt-5-nano.
seed_data_creation: Created 3 test users + sessions and 1 completed analysis (an_557dde292244) used for UI verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pipeline reliably finishes in ~60-100s with gpt-5-nano. Use longer timeouts (60s) and retries when polling during a run because the backend can return 502/timeout while LLM calls execute. Test file /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py already has these retries baked in. Frontend cookie domain creator-suite-58.preview.emergentagent.com, secure=True, sameSite=None, httpOnly=True - works with playwright add_cookies as shown.
rca of the issue: Original Anthropic key issue resolved by switching agents.py to provider='openai' model='gpt-5-nano'. New observation: backend transient 502s during pipeline are due to 6 concurrent litellm completions on one worker - mitigation is offload to thread pool or use multiple workers; not blocking correctness.
